alcWarning.title: ALC Too High
alcWarning.description: The current TX ALC has reached or exceeded 100%. Reduce the master gain now to avoid overdrive and distortion.
alcWarning.dismissHint: Click outside the popover to dismiss this reminder.

mode.loadFailed: Failed to load modes: invalid data format

audioSidecar.statusRetryingLong: Audio not ready for a while
audioSidecar.disabledHint: Unrecoverable configuration error — check audio device settings

voiceTx.switchDisabledDuringTx: Transport cannot be switched while transmitting

realtime.tokenRequestFailed: Unable to obtain a realtime access token for {{scope}}. Retry first; if it keeps failing, go to System Settings > Realtime Audio and verify the browser entrypoint and current transport path.
realtime.sessionExpired: The realtime session for {{scope}} is no longer valid. Start it again; if this keeps happening, go to System Settings > Realtime Audio and verify the entrypoint and transport policy.
realtime.publicUrlMisconfigured: {{scope}} cannot reach the currently configured realtime voice entrypoint. Go to System Settings > Realtime Audio, check the custom realtime voice entrypoint, or switch back to the default same-origin /livekit path.
realtime.signalingUnreachable: {{scope}} cannot reach the realtime voice server. Start in System Settings > Realtime Audio and confirm the current site's /livekit path is still the correct browser entrypoint; only check the custom entrypoint if you really use an extra reverse proxy or non-standard path.
realtime.iceFailed: {{scope}} reached the voice server, but media negotiation failed. Go to System Settings > Realtime Audio and check the LiveKit media network mode. If this deployment cannot expose the media ports, switch directly to Compatibility Mode (ws-compat).
realtime.noAudioTrack: {{scope}} joined the room, but no audio track was received.
realtime.audioPlaybackBlocked: Audio playback for {{scope}} was blocked by the browser.
realtime.mediaPermissionDenied: Unable to access the required media device. Please check browser or system permissions.
realtime.browserRuntimeFailed: The browser realtime audio runtime for {{scope}} failed to initialize ({{browser}}).
realtime.unknownFailure: The realtime voice path for {{scope}} failed.
realtime.compactNetworkHint: Go to System Settings > Realtime Audio first and verify the browser entrypoint plus media network mode, then re-check /livekit, ICE/TCP, and UDP connectivity.
realtime.suggestionRetryLater: Retry the last action. If needed, refresh the page or restart the app.
realtime.suggestionInteractPage: Interact with the page once, then try enabling monitoring or transmit again.
realtime.suggestionAllowMicrophone: Allow microphone access in the browser or operating system settings.
realtime.suggestionTryRecommendedBrowser: If the current browser still fails, prefer the latest Chrome or Edge for the web admin console.
realtime.suggestionCheckPublicUrl: Open System Settings > Realtime Audio first. If you enabled the advanced override, verify the custom realtime voice entrypoint there; otherwise prefer the default same-origin /livekit path.
realtime.suggestionCheckSignalingPort: Confirm the browser-facing /livekit entrypoint is reachable. If you use an extra reverse proxy, also confirm the signaling port {{port}} and path are accessible from the client.
realtime.suggestionCheckRtcTcpPort: If you want to keep using LiveKit, make sure the ICE/TCP port {{port}} is exposed. If this deployment cannot expose it, go back to System Settings > Realtime Audio and switch to Compatibility Mode (ws-compat).
realtime.suggestionCheckUdpRange: If you want to keep using LiveKit, make sure the UDP range {{range}} is reachable. If that range cannot be exposed, prefer Compatibility Mode (ws-compat) instead.
realtime.suggestionCheckDirectUrl: You can test whether this realtime voice address is reachable directly from the current client: {{url}}. If the URL itself is wrong, go back to System Settings > Realtime Audio and fix the entrypoint settings.
realtime.suggestionCheckGenericPorts: Check whether this deployment can really provide the /livekit browser entrypoint plus the ICE/TCP and UDP media ports. If not, go to System Settings > Realtime Audio and switch to Compatibility Mode (ws-compat).
realtime.transportFallbackDescription: LiveKit failed and the app already fell back to WebSocket compatibility mode. If you want LiveKit back, go to System Settings > Realtime Audio and check the browser entrypoint, media network mode, or whether this deployment should stay on compatibility mode long-term.

tuner.enableFirstDesc: Please enable the tuner switch before manual tuning

error.serialDeviceNotFound: Serial device not found. Is the radio connected via USB?
error.serialDeviceNotFoundWithPath: Serial device not found: {{path}}. Is the radio connected and powered on?
error.serialPermissionDenied: No permission to open the serial port{{- path}}. See suggestions below.
error.serialPortBusy: Serial port{{- path}} is in use by another program.
error.connectionRefused: Connection refused. The rigctld service or radio did not accept the connection.
error.networkUnreachable: Cannot reach the radio network. Check Wi-Fi / IP address.
error.radioNoResponse: The radio did not respond in time.

suggestion.connectRadioUsb: Connect the radio via USB and power it on
suggestion.refreshSerialPortList: Refresh the serial port list and pick the correct device
suggestion.serialPathHint: On macOS look for /dev/tty.usbmodem*; on Windows check the COM port number in Device Manager
suggestion.addUserToDialout: Linux: add your user to the "dialout" group, then log out and back in
suggestion.macosFilesPermission: macOS: grant terminal / app "Files and Folders" access, or launch with sudo
suggestion.windowsRunAsAdmin: Windows: run the program as administrator
suggestion.closeOtherRadioApps: Close WSJT-X / JTDX / FLDIGI or other radio control software
suggestion.reconnectUsb: Disconnect and reconnect the USB cable
suggestion.checkRigctldRunning: Check if rigctld is running at the configured host:port
suggestion.verifyRadioPoweredOn: Verify the radio is powered on
suggestion.verifyHostPort: Verify the host address and port are correct
suggestion.checkWifi: Check your Wi-Fi or network connection
suggestion.verifyRadioIpSameNetwork: Verify the radio IP address is correct and on the same network
suggestion.checkCableConnected: Verify the CAT cable is properly connected
suggestion.verifyBaudRate: Verify the baud rate matches the radio setting

on.label: Power On
on.description: Send a physical radio power-on command; TX-5DR may start the engine after the radio responds.

off.label: Power Off
off.description: Send a physical radio power-off command; TX-5DR stops related resources and disconnects after it succeeds.
off.confirm: Power off the radio? TX-5DR will stop related resources after the command succeeds.

standby.label: Standby
standby.description: Put the radio into low-power standby. After success, the CAT link may drop and TX-5DR will stop related resources.
standby.confirm: Put the radio into standby? You may need to power on again to recover.

operate.label: Operate
operate.description: Return the radio from standby to full operation.

power.compatibilityNote: Different radios respond to these commands differently. Use the command that matches your radio.

unsupported.modelUnsupported: This radio model does not support remote power control.
unsupported.networkMode: Network mode (rigctld) cannot wake the radio.
unsupported.noneMode: No radio configured for this profile.

progress.waitingStandbyDisconnect: Waiting for radio to drop CAT link…

error.notSupported: This radio does not support remote power control
error.concurrentOperation: Another power operation is already in progress

operator.cannotPickFreqDesc: Mode info not available, please try again later.
operator.noSlotDesc: No decoded data matching the current TX cycle type. Cancel TX, wait for matching cycle data, then try again.
operator.calcFailedDesc: Cannot calculate idle frequency, please try again later.
operator.allHidden: All operators hidden in client preferences
operator.standardFrequencyMultiTxDesc: This is a prohibited transmission on standard FT8/FT4 frequencies. Stop one transmitter immediately or move it to a different cycle. Callsign: {{callsigns}}.

profileModal.cannotDeleteActive: Cannot delete the currently active Profile
profileModal.icomDefaultDesc: ICOM WLAN mode uses radio audio by default; change if you have a special audio route
profileModal.subtitleList: Manage radio and audio config combinations, quickly switch between different radios
profileModal.subtitleCreate: Create a new radio configuration combination
profileModal.noProfiles: No radio configs yet, please create one

settings.title: Radio Operator Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage operator configurations and display preferences
settings.gridDesc: You can enter a 4/6/8-character grid, but FT8 transmissions always send only the first 4 characters.
settings.logSyncDesc: After configuring sync services, QSOs will be automatically uploaded to WaveLog, QRZ.com or LoTW
settings.targetSelectionPriorityModeDesc: Controls whether auto-reply prefers new DXCC entities, new grids, or new callsigns first.
settings.priorityModeBalancedDesc: Balance new grid, DXCC, callsign novelty, and signal quality.
settings.priorityModeDxccFirstDesc: Priority order: new DXCC > new grid > new callsign.
settings.priorityModeNewCallsignFirstDesc: Priority order: new callsign > new DXCC > new grid.
settings.maxQSOTimeoutDesc: While in QSO (reply received, exchanging reports), max cycles to wait for the other party to respond
settings.maxCallAttemptsDesc: When actively calling another station, max retries if they don't respond
settings.displayPrefsDesc: Choose which operators to display on this client. Disabled operators won't appear in the UI and won't receive their events.
settings.noOperatorsHint: Please create an operator in the "Operator Management" tab first
settings.hintDisabledHide: Disabled operators won't appear in the operator list
settings.hintDisabledEvents: Status updates and events for disabled operators won't be sent to this client
settings.hintLocalOnly: Settings only affect this client, not the operator running on the server
settings.hintPersisted: Settings are saved in browser local storage and will be restored on next open
settings.operatorConfigDesc: Create and manage multiple radio operator configurations
settings.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete operator "{{callsign}}"?
settings.deleteWarning: ⚠️ This action cannot be undone. All configurations for this operator will be lost.

panel.title: Radio Control
panel.noRadioMode: No radio configured — radio control is unavailable
panel.unavailableBusy: Radio is busy; try again shortly
panel.unavailableMode: Unavailable in the current radio mode
panel.unavailableRuntime: Radio reported this control is currently unavailable
panel.noSupported: The current radio reports no available controls

tuner_switch.label: Auto Tuner
tuner_switch.description: Enable or disable the radio's built-in antenna auto-tuner. When active, the radio automatically matches antenna impedance to reduce SWR and improve TX efficiency. Re-tune after changing bands or antennas.

tuner_tune.label: Manual Tune
tuner_tune.description: Trigger an immediate antenna matching cycle. Requires the auto-tuner to be enabled first. The radio will briefly transmit a low-power carrier during tuning — ensure an antenna is connected before pressing.

rf_power.label: TX Power
rf_power.description: Set transmit power from 0–100% of the radio's rated maximum. Reducing power is advisable for extended digital mode operation to prevent PA overheating; for voice, adjust freely based on propagation conditions.

af_gain.label: AF Gain
af_gain.description: Adjusts the audio output level of the radio's built-in speaker or headphone jack. This only affects the radio hardware volume, not the software monitor volume.

sql.label: Squelch
sql.description: Sets the receive squelch threshold. Higher values suppress weaker signals. For digital mode decoding, open squelch (0) is typically recommended; for voice, raise it to filter background noise as needed.

mic_gain.label: MIC Gain
mic_gain.description: Adjusts the microphone input preamplification level. Too high causes audio clipping and distortion; too low results in a weak transmitted signal. For voice, aim for the audio meter to just enter the yellow zone. Digital modes typically do not require adjustment.

compressor.label: Speech Compressor
compressor.description: Controls the radio built-in speech compressor. Enabling it can increase average modulation and perceived loudness, but too much compression adds distortion. Usually keep this off for digital modes.

compressor_level.label: Compression Level
compressor_level.description: Adjusts how strongly the speech compressor acts on transmit audio. Higher values increase average loudness but can make voice audio sound more distorted or fatiguing.

monitor_gain.label: Monitor Gain
monitor_gain.description: Adjusts the radio transmit monitor (MONI) level. A value of 0 effectively turns monitor audio off; higher values let you hear more of your own transmitted audio in the rig speaker or headphones.

nb.label: Noise Blanker
nb.description: The Noise Blanker (NB) suppresses impulse-type interference such as automotive ignition noise and industrial electrical interference. 0 means off; higher values increase blanking strength. Ineffective against continuous background noise — enable only when impulse interference is present.

nr.label: Noise Reduction
nr.description: Digital Signal Processing noise reduction (NR) reduces background white noise using DSP algorithms, improving weak signal readability. 0 means off. For FT8 and other digital mode decoding, set to 0 to avoid DSP processing affecting decode accuracy.

lock_mode.label: Panel Lock
lock_mode.description: Locks the radio front panel or tuning controls to prevent accidental changes to frequency or settings. Remote control may still work depending on the radio model.

mute.label: Mute
mute.description: Controls whether the radio local audio output is muted. This affects the speaker or headphone output on the rig, not the software decoding or network audio path.

vox.label: VOX
vox.description: Enables voice-operated transmit. When microphone audio exceeds the VOX threshold, the radio can switch to transmit automatically. Usually keep this off for digital modes.

agc_mode.label: AGC Speed
agc_mode.description: Sets the automatic gain control response speed. Faster modes react more aggressively to sudden strong signals, while slower modes often sound smoother for voice reception. Available modes depend on the radio model.

preamp.label: Preamp
preamp.description: Switches the receive preamplifier (P.AMP) level to boost weak signals. Higher gain can also amplify background noise or increase overload risk in crowded bands.

attenuator.label: Attenuator
attenuator.description: Switches the receive attenuator (ATT) level to reduce front-end overload in strong-signal environments. Higher values apply stronger attenuation.

mode_bandwidth.label: Mode Bandwidth
mode_bandwidth.description: Reads and switches the active filter bandwidth for the current operating mode. The voice spectrum overlay prefers this real bandwidth when available.

rit_offset.label: RIT Offset
rit_offset.description: Adjusts the receive incremental tuning offset without changing transmit frequency. Useful for fine-tuning received audio pitch.

xit_offset.label: XIT Offset
xit_offset.description: Adjusts the transmit incremental tuning offset without changing receive frequency. Useful when TX and RX need to be separated slightly.

tuning_step.label: Tuning Step
tuning_step.description: Sets the step size used by tuning controls. Smaller steps help with precise tuning, while larger steps make it easier to move quickly across a band.

repeater_shift.label: Repeater Shift
repeater_shift.description: Sets the repeater duplex direction: no offset, minus offset, or plus offset. Usually used together with the repeater offset value.

repeater_offset.label: Repeater Offset
repeater_offset.description: Sets the repeater frequency offset. The UI displays this in kHz while the radio interface uses raw Hz values.

ctcss_tone.label: CTCSS Tone
ctcss_tone.description: Selects the continuous tone-coded squelch system (CTCSS) tone frequency used for repeater access or squelch control.

dcs_code.label: DCS Code
dcs_code.description: Selects the digital-coded squelch (DCS) code used to match repeater or radio network squelch settings.
